mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card behavior on Print and Payment History page when a plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages, additional pages, progress bars, tooltips, and page count displays.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires printer enrollment functionality via API or UI. Batch 15 contains ShipmentTrackingPage (for tracking shipments), ShippingBillingPage (for managing shipping addresses and payment methods), SignInPage (for authentication), ThankYouPage (confirmation page), and TermsOfServiceHPSmartPage (consent page). None of these page objects provide printer enrollment functionality. The enrollment methods would likely be in an enrollment page object, dashboard page object, or API helper module, which are not present in this batch.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires API methods to retrieve and verify subscription details. Batch 15 contains only page objects for UI interactions (shipment tracking, shipping/billing management, sign-in, thank you, terms of service). No API helper classes or subscription management utilities are present in this batch. Subscription verification methods would be in API helper modules or subscription management classes not included in this batch.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires API methods to check subscription status. Batch 15 contains only UI page objects without subscription status verification capabilities. The ShippingBillingPage manages payment methods but does not provide subscription status checking. API helpers for subscription status verification are not present in this batch.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires UI elements and methods for pausing a plan, likely on a dashboard or plan management page. Batch 15 contains ShipmentTrackingPage, ShippingBillingPage, SignInPage, ThankYouPage, and TermsOfServiceHPSmartPage. None of these pages contain pause plan buttons, CIT modal elements, or Pause Instant Ink links. The pause plan functionality would be on a dashboard or plan management page object not present in this batch.

test_case_mappings.no_match_reason:
Test case C44873414 tests the Billing Cycle Period card behavior on the Print and Payment History page during HP Instant Ink plan pause scenarios. The test requires: (1) PrintAndPaymentHistoryPage with locators for billing cycle card, plan pause information, complimentary pages progress bar, additional pages progress bar, tooltips, and page count displays; (2) DashboardPage with pause plan functionality; (3) API helpers for printer enrollment, subscription management, time manipulation (32-day shift), billing charge triggering, and page printing simulation; (4) Progress bar interaction methods for color verification and fill percentage checking; (5) Tooltip interaction methods with responsive behavior (hover for desktop, click for mobile); (6) Verification methods for progress bar colors (black for complimentary, yellow for additional), text values, and visibility states.

Batch 15 contains five page objects focused on completely different functional areas:

1. ShipmentTrackingPage (shipment_tracking_page.py): Manages ink/toner cartridge and paper shipment tracking with FAQ sections, tracking cards for different cartridge colors (K, C, M, Y, CMY), shipment history table, and support links. This page is for TRACKING SHIPMENTS AFTER THEY ARE SENT, not for managing billing cycles or viewing page usage during plan pause. No overlap with test requirements.

2. ShippingBillingPage (shipping_billing_page.py): Manages shipping addresses and payment methods with form modals for address entry, payment card input, business account selection, and address validation. This page is for MANAGING WHERE TO SHIP and HOW TO PAY, not for viewing billing cycle information or page usage tracking. The billing section here refers to payment method management, not billing cycle period cards. No overlap with test requirements.

3. SignInPage (sign_in_page.py): Provides authentication with email/username input, password input, and sign-in button. This is a LOGIN PAGE with no connection to billing cycles, plan pause, or page usage tracking. No overlap with test requirements.

4. ThankYouPage (thank_you_page.py): Confirmation page shown after completing enrollment or setup processes, displaying thank you message, email confirmation, cartridge image, and continue button. This is a POST-ENROLLMENT CONFIRMATION PAGE, not related to ongoing billing cycle monitoring or plan pause scenarios. No overlap with test requirements.

5. TermsOfServiceHPSmartPage (tos_hp_smart_page.py): Simple consent page with a single continue button for accepting HP Smart terms of service. This is a LEGAL CONSENT PAGE with no connection to billing cycles or subscription management. No overlap with test requirements.

The test case requires components for:
- Print and Payment History page (NOT in this batch)
- Billing cycle period card elements (NOT in this batch)
- Plan pause information display (NOT in this batch)
- Complimentary pages progress bar (NOT in this batch)
- Additional pages progress bar (NOT in this batch)
- Tooltip elements and interaction methods (NOT in this batch)
- Dashboard with pause plan functionality (NOT in this batch)
- API helpers for enrollment, time shift, billing, page printing (NOT in this batch)
- Progress bar color and fill verification methods (NOT in this batch)

Conclusion: There is zero functional overlap between the test case requirements and the components in batch 15. All page objects in this batch serve different purposes (shipment tracking, address/payment management, authentication, confirmation, consent) and contain no elements or methods relevant to billing cycle period card testing, plan pause scenarios, or page usage tracking. The required page objects and API helpers for this test case are in different batches containing HP Instant Ink dashboard, print history, and subscription management components.
